pr.title: Attempt to fix doc comments and todo's
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Large documentation-formatting pass that converted many ordinary C/C++ comments and TODO markers into Doxygen-visible forms, mainly using /// and /// @todo. The author described it as a rough first pass whose goal was to get comments into Doxygen even if it produced false positives. The PR touched 230 files across MSL, MetroTRK, common headers, camera/debug camera, effects, fighters, items, stages, menus, map/collision, and baselib. Evidence suggests this was primarily a documentation/header hygiene change, not a semantic code or matching change, though it preserved several existing matching notes such as math_data preventing address hoisting and comments about fake matches, stack allocation, pragma inlining, and enum-size issues.
what_changed: Converted many line comments from // to Doxygen-style /// so they become visible to generated documentation.
Converted TODO comments to Doxygen task syntax, for example // TODO: to /// @todo and //! TODO: to /// @todo.

Applied the same Doxygen treatment to macro/enum section headers in MetroTRK headers, such as memory command options, register command options, step command options, reply errors, special purpose registers, and MSR register bits.

Made existing explanatory matching comments Doxygen-visible in MSL math files, including notes that math lookup tables live in math_data.c to prevent the compiler from hoisting array base addresses and that logf Taylor coefficients are stored as u32 bit patterns for lwz loads.
Updated common_structs.h comments for sound effects, HSD_PAD, collision flags, and TODO notes such as PascalCase member naming and possible bitfield structs.

Updated camera/debug-camera comments for named behaviors like Camera_PauseZoom, Camera_PauseRotate, Camera_SetModeTraining, Camera_ToScreen, Camera_IsSubjectOnScreen, Debug_UpdateCamera, Debug_OrbitAroundInterest, Debug_UpdateFreecamRotation, Debug_UpdateFreecamDolly, and Debug_UpdateFreecamPosition.

Preserved several decomp TODOs while making them Doxygen-visible, including enum-size issues in MetroTRK msgbuf_t, fake matching in TRK support, possible padding/file-order questions in dbcamera, avoiding auto-inlining in dbitem, and camera stack-allocation matching trouble.
Removed at least one large stale commented-out duplicate struct sketch for CameraUnkGlobals in src/melee/cm/types.h, leaving the active struct definition.
Reformatted/commented some commented-out prototypes or code fragments as Doxygen comments, including effect async prototypes and static data sketches.
Touched a very broad set of fighter, item, stage, menu, map, effect, library, and baselib files with mostly add/delete-balanced comment-only hunks.
smart_moves: Used Doxygen-native /// @todo instead of plain TODO so unresolved decomp work can be indexed and surfaced by documentation tooling.

Kept important matching rationale attached to code rather than deleting it, especially comments about preventing compiler address hoisting, forcing lwz loads from globals, fake matches, inlining pragmas, enum size issues, and stack allocation side effects.
Converted section-heading comments in headers and low-level libraries, which helps Doxygen organize constants, enums, and struct notes without requiring immediate symbol renaming or code movement.

Preserved uncertainty in comments rather than overclaiming; examples include notes like 'probably was a bandaid', 'does the padding mean this should be in another file', and 'port should probably be a u8, but it is difficult'.
The change appears intentionally low-risk for matching because most hunks are comment-only and line additions/deletions are largely balanced across many files.
decomp_lessons: For documentation passes, prefer Doxygen-visible TODO syntax so unresolved matching, typing, and struct-layout questions become searchable across the project.
Do not strip comments that explain codegen constraints; notes about address hoisting, lwz loads, fake matches, inlining, and stack allocation are useful future matching breadcrumbs.
Uncertain type/ABI notes should stay near the affected declarations, such as enum-size shenanigans in protocol structs or whether a parameter should be u8 but is kept wider for matching.
Comment-only cleanup can still improve decomp workflow if it makes technical debt visible to tooling and future contributors.
Be cautious when converting commented-out code blocks to Doxygen comments: it can expose false positives, which the PR body explicitly accepts as a rough first pass.
Large cross-tree documentation passes should be treated as documentation hygiene unless there is explicit evidence of semantic code edits; here the evidence mostly shows comment conversion.
naming_conventions: Descriptive plain comments were normalized toward /// Doxygen line comments.

Existing decomp names such as Camera_PauseZoom, Camera_PauseRotate, Camera_SetModeTraining, Camera_ToScreen, Camera_IsSubjectOnScreen, Debug_UpdateCamera, and Debug_UpdateFreecamPosition were preserved as documentation labels rather than renamed symbols.
common_structs.h retained a TODO that HSD_PAD members should use PascalCase.
The PR did not provide strong evidence of new symbol naming policy beyond Doxygen comment syntax normalization.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: MSL math comments document that keeping lookup tables in math_data.c prevents the compiler from hoisting array base addresses, which is needed to match the original code.
MSL math_data comments document storing logf Taylor coefficients as u32 bit patterns so they can be loaded with lwz and reinterpreted as floats.
MetroTRK support retained a Doxygen @todo noting a fake match around TRKRequestSend.
camera.c retained a Doxygen-visible TODO that one branch could not be made to use the same logic as another without causing stack allocation differences.
dbitem.c retained a Doxygen @todo to avoid auto-inlining into fn_CheckItemAndPokemonMenu.
eflib.c retained comments around pragmas and a possible inline returning ret_gobj, but evidence only shows documentation conversion, not a new matching solution.
msghndlr.h retained an enum-size TODO inside msgbuf_t, highlighting ABI/layout concerns.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The only stated rationale is from the PR body: it was a rough first pass intended to get comments into Doxygen even if it created false positives.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining plain TODO comments that were not converted to /// @todo after PR2164.
Search for Doxygen false positives caused by commented-out prototypes or code blocks converted to ///.
Audit @todo entries mentioning fake match, stack allocation, enum size, and auto-inlining for active matching work.
Check whether Doxygen output now indexes comments in large generated/static headers such as camera.static.h and gmallstar.static.h.
Review whether commented-out code blocks should use @code blocks or stay non-Doxygen comments to avoid noisy documentation.
